de.uka.ilkd.key.nparser.varexp

Interface ConditionBuilder

  • All Superinterfaces:
    TacletBuilderCommand
    All Known Implementing Classes:
    AbstractConditionBuilder, ConstructorBasedBuilder

    public interface ConditionBuilder
extends TacletBuilderCommand
    A specilized TacletBuilderCommand for handling \varconds.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods Default Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      default void apply(TacletBuilder<?> tacletBuilder, java.lang.Object[] arguments, java.util.List<java.lang.String> parameters, boolean negated)
      This method will add the contructed VariableCondition to given tacletBuilder.
      VariableCondition build(java.lang.Object[] arguments, java.util.List<java.lang.String> parameters, boolean negated)
      Should construct a variable condition for the given arguments and parameters.

      • Methods inherited from interface de.uka.ilkd.key.nparser.varexp.TacletBuilderCommand

        getArgumentTypes, isSuitableFor

    • Method Detail

      • build

        VariableCondition build(java.lang.Object[] arguments,
                        java.util.List<java.lang.String> parameters,
                        boolean negated)
        Should construct a variable condition for the given arguments and parameters. The arguments are the adhering the type specified in TacletBuilderCommand.getArgumentTypes().

        For a varcond \varcond(\abc[p1,p2](a1, a2)) the arguments are a1 and a2, the parameters are p1 and p2. negated is true if \not is used.

      • apply

        default void apply(TacletBuilder<?> tacletBuilder,
                   java.lang.Object[] arguments,
                   java.util.List<java.lang.String> parameters,
                   boolean negated)
        This method will add the contructed VariableCondition to given tacletBuilder.
        Specified by:
        apply in interface TacletBuilderCommand
        See Also:
        TacletBuilderCommand.apply(TacletBuilder, Object[], List, boolean)
